items.title: Enrich Source Metadata at the Retrieval Boundary

exceptions.reason: Package-level requirements are covered by the ingestion and retrieval feature specs. Verification will be added as those features are implemented.

requirements.statement: The system shall accept markdown files as knowledge sources and make their content available for retrieval.
The system shall provide a Q&A interface for asking natural language questions and receiving AI-generated answers from ingested knowledge.
The system shall support work and personal segments, keeping all knowledge and query results scoped to the selected segment.
The UI shall display the AI-generated answer after the question is submitted.
The UI shall indicate that a query is in progress while waiting for a response.
The UI shall allow the user to choose between ask mode (AI-generated answer) and search mode (raw retrieved passages), defaulting to ask.
The UI shall provide a text input for the user to enter a natural language question.
The UI shall allow the user to select a segment (work or personal) before submitting a question.
The system shall accept markdown files as input for ingestion.
The system shall chunk and embed document content via arcana and persist embeddings to pgvector.
Re-ingesting a document with the same source path and segment shall replace its existing embeddings rather than duplicate them.
Each ingested document shall be tagged with a segment (work or personal) at ingestion time.
The system shall make ingestion failures and partial failures inspectable to callers rather than failing silently.
The system shall record when a source was last successfully ingested.
The system shall support reprocessing a previously ingested source after failure or source change.
The system shall detect when a source has changed since its last successful ingestion.
The system shall record an ingestion status for each ingested source so successful and failed ingestion attempts are distinguishable.
The system shall accept a natural language question and a segment as input.
The system shall enrich each retrieved passage with segment, source file path, excerpt text, relevance score, and chunk index before returning it to callers.
The system shall return an AI-generated answer assembled from the most relevant retrieved passages.
Retrieval shall return only content belonging to the specified segment; work and personal knowledge shall not mix.
The system shall display supporting citations alongside each AI-generated answer.
Each ask-mode citation shall display source-identifying metadata, excerpt text, segment, and ordinal rank.
Citations shall make the active segment visible so users can confirm the answer was drawn from the intended knowledge scope.
Citations shall be rendered in descending retrieval relevance order so the displayed rank matches retrieval priority.
The system shall expose a lightweight relevance signal for each supporting citation using ordinal rank within the retrieved result set.
When an answer is based on multiple retrieved passages, the retrieval layer shall return them as a list of distinct source entries.
Each citation shall carry the retrieved passage text used as supporting context.
Each citation shall include enough metadata to identify its origin, including source file path or title and segment.
Each search result citation shall display the same core fields as ask-mode citations: source-identifying metadata, segment, excerpt text, and ordinal rank.
Search mode shall present results using the same citation-card structure used for ask-mode citations.
When zero supporting sources are returned, the system shall still display the answer rather than suppressing it.
If ask mode returns an answer with zero supporting sources, the system shall clearly indicate that the answer is weakly grounded.
The Sources section shall render an explicit empty-state message when zero supporting sources are available.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When planning and verification run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

given: A source has been ingested successfully
then: The system can determine that the source changed after the last successful ingestion
The source is represented with a failure status
Failure information is available for inspection

given: A source previously failed ingestion or is marked changed
then: The system attempts ingestion again for that source

given: The user asks a question in ask mode and relevant passages are retrieved
then: The UI shows the synthesized answer and a Sources section listing each citation with source-identifying metadata, segment, excerpt text, and ordinal rank

given: Retrieved passages are available for an answer or search result
then: Each citation displays its ordinal rank and the highest-ranked citation appears first

given: The user searches in search mode
then: Each result includes source-identifying metadata and an excerpt
Each result displays segment and ordinal rank
Results are rendered with the same citation layout used for ask-mode citations

given: The user asks a question and answer generation succeeds
then: The Sources section displays a clear "No supporting sources available" message
The empty Sources state is visible instead of an empty citation list

meta.summary: LiveView page for querying the knowledge base, with segment selection, ask/search mode toggle (defaulting to ask), and result display. Results are fetched asynchronously via start_async and rendered on completion.

Accepts markdown files, chunks and embeds them via arcana, persists embeddings with segment tagging to pgvector, and records ingestion health including status, timestamps, and content hashes for change detection.
The system tracks ingestion status, recency, failures, and source changes so users can inspect ingestion health and reprocess sources when needed.

Personal Phoenix LiveView second brain that ingests markdown documents with health tracking, answers natural language questions scoped to a work or personal segment, and surfaces grounded citations for every response.

Accepts natural language questions scoped to a segment and returns AI-generated answers alongside enriched source maps containing passage text, file path, segment, score, and chunk position, sorted by descending relevance score.
The chat UI displays a Sources list beneath each AI-generated answer, showing source identity, segment, excerpt, and ordinal rank for each supporting passage.
Each supporting citation carries a lightweight relevance signal expressed as retrieval rank so users can judge how strongly a passage supported the answer without interpreting raw similarity scores.
The retrieval layer surfaces source metadata, excerpt text, and score for each retrieved passage, sorted by descending relevance score, so the UI can present grounded citations in rank order.

Search mode results display source metadata, excerpt text, segment visibility, and rank using the same citation card structure as ask-mode citations, giving users a uniform inspection experience across both modes.
When ask mode produces an answer without any retrieved supporting passages, the UI presents a clear weak-grounding state instead of implying the answer is well supported.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.
